title: Cron Parser
description: Paste a cron expression to get a plain-language explanation, a field-by-field breakdown, and a forecast of upcoming run times.

notice.title: Parser, not a generator
notice.body: This tool translates an existing cron expression into plain language and predicts when it will next fire. It supports the standard 5 fields (minute hour day month weekday), ranges/lists/steps, English month and weekday abbreviations, 7 for Sunday, and common aliases like @daily and @hourly.

input.label: Cron expression
input.format: Format: minute hour day month weekday

items.monthlyFirst: Midnight on the 1st of each month

summary.heading: Plain language
summary.union: Note: both the day-of-month and day-of-week fields are restricted, so cron uses their union — it fires when either one matches.

nextRuns.heading: Upcoming runs
nextRuns.timezoneNote: Computed in your current time zone (local time).

dom.stepRange: every {{step}} days from day {{from}} through {{to}} of the month
dom.range: from day {{from}} through {{to}} of the month

dow.stepRange: every {{step}} days of the week from {{from}} through {{to}}

errors.title: Could not parse the expression
errors.fieldCount: Expected 5 fields (minute hour day month weekday), got {{count}}.
errors.rangeOrder: Range start {{from}} cannot be greater than end {{to}}.
errors.invalidStep: Invalid step "{{value}}"; the step must be an integer greater than 0.
